core_mechanism: a self-reinforcing local attachment rule in a growing graph where new links preferentially connect elements that already share a common neighbor (triadic/neighbor-of-neighbor closure), amplifying initial stochastic density fluctuations into emergent clustered mesoscale structure, with a fitness-heterogeneity control parameter driving a phase transition between clustered and hub-dominated regimes
brief_reason: The abstract states that network-growth models based on simple triadic closure spontaneously produce community structure from initial stochastic heterogeneity in link concentration, and that tuning a fitness-based link attractivity yields a phase transition where communities give way to superhub organization.
